hotato

Open, offline turn-taking eval for voice agents.
suite: barge-instack genericengine barge_scoring (vendored, MIT) 0.1.0offline yes
0 of 2
events pass
REGRESSION
calleragenttalk-overyield
Bad-agent demo: the agent talks straight over an addressed 2.0s interruption (should yield, did not)
fd-01-missed-interruptionshould_yield
FAIL
expected yieldtodid yield no
The exact audio the scorer measured, embedded in this file. Nothing is fetched.
scenario audiofd-01-missed-interruption.example.wav
caller onset2.00s
time to yieldn/a
talk-over2.65s
response gapn/a
premature startn/a
  • expected the agent to yield but it kept talking
fix [config] Missed interruption: the agent kept talking over the caller
The caller took the floor and the agent never stopped within the search window. Increase interruption sensitivity so a genuine floor-taking event registers.

Real call: soft backchannel halts the agent mid-word for good (Vapi defaults)
fd-02-backchannel-yieldedshould_not_yield
FAIL
expected holdtodid yield yes
The exact audio the scorer measured, embedded in this file. Nothing is fetched.
scenario audiofd-02-backchannel-yielded.example.wav
caller onset2.00s
time to yield0.34s
talk-over0.32s
response gapn/a
premature startn/a
  • expected the agent to keep the floor but it yielded (a false or phantom barge-in)
fix [engagement-control] False barge-in: a backchannel was treated as a bid for the floor
The caller only signalled 'I'm listening' (mhm / right / okay) but the agent stopped mid-thought. You can raise a words-to-interrupt threshold, but the same threshold that ignores 'mhm' will also ignore 'stop'. This is a discrimination problem, not a threshold problem.
frame inspector: 800 frames, hop 0.010s, noise floor caller -120.0 dBFS / agent -120.0 dBFS

Thresholds used
Every value the scorer read. Same audio and config reproduce every number above.
frame_ms20.0
hop_ms10.0
yield_hangover_sec0.2
max_search_sec3.0
caller_proximity_sec0.5
turn_end_silence_sec0.2
premature_tolerance_sec0.05
VAD parametercalleragent
rel_db15.015.0
abs_gate_db-60.0-60.0
hangover_sec0.150.15
noise_percentile0.10.1
dyn_margin_db22.022.0
Method. energy-based VAD framing over aligned caller/agent channels; three objective timing signals (did_yield, seconds_to_yield, talk_over). Deterministic given the same audio and config, with an explicit ceiling; every threshold above is an exposed parameter and every frame is inspectable. No accuracy score. What this measures, and what it does not.
